Section · the short version
COLOPHON / who is writing, and from where

One person, in public

Hi. I'm Bas — a systems-curious engineer working out loud. This page is the long-form version of my email signature.

PADOVA · UTC+1· OPEN FOR WORK· <a href='#contact'>JUMP TO CONTACT</a>
PORTRAIT · HAND-SET · 2025

— fig. 1 · self
AT A GLANCE
NameBasliel B. Gugsa (Basz)
RoleSystems engineer in the making, late-night hobbyist
BasePadova, IT · UTC+1
StackBash · Rust · C · Python · Zig · nix · Linux · mutt
StatusOpen to collaborations.

I write software the slow way, on purpose. My days start with mutt and a pot of coffee and end, most nights, with a git commit I will probably rewrite tomorrow. I work on systems — the layer of software where abstractions stop being free and start costing cycles, memory, and sometimes sleep. I like it there. I like that you can measure things.

Professionally I spend my time on backends, developer tooling, and the occasional compiler. For the last three years I've been a systems engineer at a small infrastructure company — the kind of place where the code review catches a dangling mmap before the test suite does. Before that I studied at TU Delft, dropped out to contribute to an open-source OS, came back, and graduated by writing a thesis on memory allocator design nobody asked for.

What I work on

Four recurring interests that show up in both paid work and projects. One: operating systems, especially the gap between what POSIX promises and what Linux actually does. Two: small, legible programs — the kind you can read in an afternoon and feel smarter afterwards. Three: developer tools that respect the keyboard. Four: local-first software, and the quiet case that it's still the best default for most things.

How I work

I write notes as I go — it's the only way I've found to think at the pace of the problem. I build in public when I can. I am allergic to meetings that could have been a thread, and devoted to meetings that are properly chaired.

"Make it work, make it right, make it fast" — in that order, but with honesty about which one you've reached.

Outside the terminal

I ride a steel bike with too many lights. I read more than I finish — currently A Philosophy of Software Design (again). I cook slowly, and badly.

Currently
Buildingthis site
ReadingA Philosophy of Software Design
ListeningWilliam Basinski — on repeat
LearningZig, patiently
Elsewhere

Get in touch

I read every message. I reply to most of them. Ask me about compilers, collaborations, or weird shell scripts.

· COLOPHON ·
Made with intent,
not for scale.

Stack

Astro · MDX · Sass · vanilla JS. No frameworks. No analytics. Static output on Cloudflare Pages.

Design

Brutalist print zine aesthetic. Anybody (display) · Playfair Display (serif) · DM Mono. No rounded corners. No gradients on UI.

Philosophy

Build in public. Own your words. No paywalls, no newsletters, no tracking pixels. Just pages.

· bas.dev · Padova · 2026 · Static · No JS required for reading · Built with intent ·
